questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: $K_a$ for $\mathrm{HCN}$ is $5 \times 10^{-10}$ at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. For maintaining a constant $\mathrm{pH}=9$, the volume of $5 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{KCN}$ solution required to be added to $10 \mathrm{~mL}$ of $2 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{HCN}$ solution is
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{pH}=\mathrm{p} K_a+\log \frac{[\text { Salt }]}{[\text { Acid }]}$
Let the volume of $\mathrm{KCN}$ solution required be $V \mathrm{~mL}$ $\therefore \quad[\mathrm{KCN}]=\frac{5 \times V}{V+10}$ and $[\mathrm{HCN}]=\frac{10 \times 2}{V+10}$ Now from eqn. (i), $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{pH}=-\log \left(5 \times 10^{-10}\right)+\log \left[\frac{5 \times V}{V+10} / \frac{10 \times 2}{V+10}\right] \\ & 9=-\log \left(5 \times 10^{-10}\right)+\log \frac{V}{4}\end{aligned}$ On solving, $V=1.99 \approx 2 \mathrm{~mL}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q:
The percentage hydrolysis of $0.15 \mathrm{M}$ solution of ammonium acetate, $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{a}}$ for $\mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{COOH}$ is $1.8 \times 10^{-5}$
and $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{b}}$ for $\mathrm{NH}_{3}$ is $1.8 \times 10^{-5}$
questions.solution: $\alpha=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{w}}}{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{a}} \times \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{b}}}}=\sqrt{\frac{1 \times 10^{-14}}{1.8 \times 10^{-5} \times 1.8 \times 10^{-5}}}=0.55$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: $2.5 \mathrm{ml}$ of $0.4(\mathrm{M})$ weak monoacidic base $\left(\mathrm{k}_{\mathrm{b}}=1 \times 10^{-12}\right.$ at $\left.25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\right)$ is titrated with $\frac{2}{15}(\mathrm{M}) \mathrm{HCl}$ in water at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. The concentration of $\mathrm{H}^{+}$at equivalence point is $\left(\mathrm{k}_{\mathrm{w}}=1 \times 10^{-14}\right.$, at $\left.25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\right)$,
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{N}_{1} \mathrm{~V}_{1}=\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{~V}_{2}$ (At equivalence point)
$0.4 \times 2.5=\frac{2}{15} \times \mathrm{V}_{2}$ 
$\mathrm{BOH}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{HCl}(\mathrm{aq}) \rightarrow \mathrm{B} \mathrm{Cl}^{-}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(I)$
$\therefore \mathrm{V}_{2}=\frac{0.4 \times 2.5}{\frac{2}{15}}=\frac{0.4 \times 2.5}{2} \times 15=7.5 \mathrm{ml}$
$\therefore$ Concentration of salt $=\frac{0.4 \times 2.5}{2.5+7.5}$
or, Salt of weak base and strong acid = $0.1$
$\therefore\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]=\mathrm{Ch}=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{w}} \mathrm{C}}{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{b}}}}=\sqrt{\frac{10^{-14} \times 0.1}{10^{-12}}}=\sqrt{10^{-3}}=3.2 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{M}$
as $C h^{2}=\frac{K_{w}}{K_{b}} \quad \therefore h=\sqrt{\frac{K_{w}}{K_{b} C}}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: The molar solubility product is $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{sp}}$. ' $\mathrm{s}$ ' is given in terms of $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{sp}}$ by the relation
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{Ksp}=108 s^5$
$1 \times 4^4 \times s^{1+4}=256 s^5=\mathrm{Ksp}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The $\mathrm{pKa}$ of a weak acid $(\mathrm{HA})$ is $4.5$. The $\mathrm{pOH}$ of an aqueous buffered solution of $\mathrm{HA}$ in which $50 \%$ of the acid is ionized is
questions.solution:
For buffer solution
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{pH}=\mathrm{pK}_{\mathrm{a}}+\log \frac{[\text { Salt }]}{[\text { Acid }]} \\
& =4.5+\log \frac{[\text { Salt }]}{[\text { Acid }]}
\end{aligned}$
as $\mathrm{HA}$ is $50 \%$ ionized so $[\text{Salt}]$=$[\text{Acid}]$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{pH}=4.5 \\
& \mathrm{pH}+\mathrm{pOH}=14 \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{pOH}=14-4.5=9.5
\end{aligned}$
Hence (C) is correct.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A substance containing hydrogen and releasing $\mathrm{H}^{+}$in aqueous medium is acid. Identify theory suggesting this concept, from following.
questions.solution:
According to Arrhenius theory an acid is a
Substance that contains hydrogen and gives $\mathrm{H}^{+}$ions in an aqueous solution.
- $\quad$ Lewis acid $\rightarrow$ lone pair acceptor
- $\quad$ Bronsted-Lowry acid is a substance which donates a proton or $\mathrm{H}^{+}$ion to the other compound and forms a conjugated base.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
A solution is 0.1M in Cl- and 0.001M in CrO42-.
Solid AgNO3 is gradually added to it Assuming that the addition does not change in volume and Ksp(AgCl)=1.7×10-10M2 and KspAg2CrO4=1.9×10-12M3.
Select correct statement from the following:
options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>AgCl</mi></math> precipitates first because its <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mi>sp</mi></msub></math> is high.

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Ag</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>CrO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> precipitates first as its <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mi>sp</mi></msub></math> is low.

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Ag</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>CrO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> precipitates first because the amount of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Ag</mi><mo>+</mo></msup></math> needed is low.

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>AgCl</mi></math> will precipitate first as the amount of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Ag</mi><mo>+</mo></msup></math> needed to precipitate is low.
questions.solution:
i Ag+ required to ppt AgCls

Ksp=IP=Ag+Cl-=1.7×10-10

Ag+=1.7×10-9

ii Ag+ required to ppt Ag2CrO4s

Ksp=IP=[Ag+]2CrO4-2=1.9×10-12

Ag+=4.3×10-5

Ag+ required to ppt AgCl is low so AgCl will ppt 1st.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: The <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>pH</mi></math> of an aqueous solution containing <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math> benzoic acid <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>pK</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>20</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math> sodium benzoate is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>.</mo></math> The volume of benzoic acid solution in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>300</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of this buffer solution is __________<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>mL</mi></math>.
questions.solution:
Benzoic acid+Sodium benzoateMolarity1M1MVolumeVamlVsmlmilli molesVa×1Vs×1

pH=4.5

pKa=4.20

pH=pKa+log[ salt ][ acid ]

4.5=4.2+logVsVaWhere,Vs- Volume of saltVa- Volume of acid

VsVa=2..........(i)

Vs+Va=300........(ii)

Va=100ml

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Solubility of calcium phosphate (molecular mass, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math>) in water is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">W</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></msub></math> per <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo>.</mo></math> Its solubility product at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> will be approximately.
questions.solution:
Given the solubility of Ca3PO42 is Wg/100 mL. The solubility in mol/L is S=WM×1000100mol/L.

The equilibrium reaction of calcium phosphate is 

Ca3PO42⇌3Ca2++2PO43-At equilibrium3S2S

Now, Ksp=Ca2+3PO43-2

⇒Ksp=3S32S2=108×S5

⇒Ksp=108×WM×105⇒Ksp=108×105×WM5

=1.08×107WM5
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A weak acid of dissociation constant $10^{-5}$ is being titrated with aqueous $\mathrm{NaOH}$ solution. The $\mathrm{pH}$ at the point of one-third neutralisation of the acid will be
questions.solution:
Hints: $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{a}}=10^{-5} \Rightarrow \mathrm{pK}_{\mathrm{a}}=-\log \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{a}}=-\log 10^{-5}=5$
$$
\begin{array}{llcc} 
& \mathrm{HA}+\mathrm{NaOH} \longrightarrow \mathrm{NaA}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\
\text { Initial } & 1 \mathrm{~mole} 0 & 0 & 0 \\
\text { Final } & (1-1 / 3) \mathrm{mole} & 1 / 3 \mathrm{~mole} & 1 / 3 \mathrm{~mole} \\
& =2 / 3 \mathrm{~mole} & 1 / 3 \mathrm{~mole}
\end{array}
$$
(Assumed weak acid to be monoprotic, since only one dissociation constant value is provided)
Final solution acts as an acidic buffer.
$$
\Rightarrow \mathrm{pH}=\mathrm{pK}_{\mathrm{a}}+\log \frac{[\text { salt }]}{[\text { Acid }]} \Rightarrow \mathrm{pH}=5+\log \frac{\frac{1}{3}}{\frac{2}{3}}=5+\log \frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow \mathrm{pH}=5-\log 2
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
The titration curve of weak acid vs. strong base with phenolphthalein as indicator is shown below. The Kphenolphthalein =4×10-10

Given: log 2 = 0.3

The number of following statement/s which is/are correct about phenolphthalein is _______

A. It can be used as an indicator for the titration of weak acid with weak base.

B. It begins to change colour at pH = 8.4

C. It is a weak organic base

D. It is colourless in acidic medium
questions.solution:
Phenolphthalein is an organic acid and can be represented as HPh.

HPh (colourless) ⇌H++Ph- (Pink) 

Using Henderson equation for phenolphthalein

pH=pKIn+logPh-[HPh]

At equivalence point  Ph-=[HPh]

∴pH2=pKIn=-log[4]+10=-0.6+10=9.4

Hence at (9.4±1)PH , phenolphthalein starts changing colour. Phenolphthalein is colourless in acidic medium and pink in basic medium. Phenolphthalein indicator distinguish the pH change between 8 to 10. Therefore, it is used for strong acid and strong base titration or weak acid and strong base titration. Hence, A and C are incorrect statements.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Incorrect statement for the use of indicator in acid-base titration is:
options: Methyl orange may be used for a weak acid vs weak bas titration.
Phenolphthalein may be used for a strong acid vs strong base titration.
Methyl orange is a suitable indicator for a strong acid vs weak base titration.
Phenolphthalein is a suitable indicator for a weak acid vs strong base titration.
questions.solution:
Methyl orange and phenolphthalein indicators work with the change in pH and are termed as acid–base indicators.

Methy lorange is sutable indicator for strong acid vs strong base and strong acid vs weak base titration.

Phenolphthalein is sutable indicator for strong acid vs strong base and weak acid vs strong base titration.

There is no suitable indicator that can be used in the titration of weak acid and weak base. So, the correct option is A.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.solution:
lnS = lnA− ΔH RT
Slope =  −ΔH RT
lnS vs  1 T graph with negative slope.
So, ΔH>0
Salts with water of crystallisation.
i.e., CuSO4.5H2O .

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A 0.02 M solution of pyridinium hydrochloride has pH=3.44. Calculate the ionization constant of pyridine.
questions.solution:
C 5 H 5 N · HCl + H 2 O ⇌ C 5 H 5 N + Cl - + H 3 O +

K a = C 5 H 5 N + Cl - H 3 O + C 5 H 5 N · HCl

pH=3.44=-logH3O+

log H 3 O + = - 3.44 = 4 ¯ .56

H 3 O + = antilog 4 ¯ .56 = 3.63 × 10 - 4 M

H3O+=C5H5NCl-=3.63×10-4

Hence, Ka=3.63×10-4×3.63×10-40.02=6.588×10-6

      pKa=-log Ka=-log 6.588×10-6pKa=6+(-0.8187)=5.18pKa+pKb=14pKb=14-5.18=8.82-log Kb=+8.82

or log Kb=-8.82=9¯.18

  Kb=antilog9¯.18=1.5×10-9.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
Values of dissociation constant, $K_a$ are given as follows :
$\begin{array}{lc}\text { Acid } & \boldsymbol{K}_{\boldsymbol{a}} \\ \mathrm{HCN} & 6.2 \times 10^{-10} \\ \mathrm{HF} & 7.2 \times 10^{-4} \\ \mathrm{HNO}_2 & 4.0 \times 10^{-4}\end{array}$
Correct order of increasing base strength of the base $\mathrm{CN}^{-}, \mathrm{F}^{-}$and $\mathrm{NO}_2^{-}$will be :
questions.solution:
Higher the value of $\mathrm{K}_a$ lower will be the value of $\mathrm{pK}_{\mathrm{a}}$ i.e. higher will be the acidic nature. Further since $\mathrm{CN}^{-}, \mathrm{F}^{-}$and $\mathrm{NO}_2^{-}$are conjugate base of the acids $\mathrm{HCN}, \mathrm{HF}$ and $\mathrm{HNO}_2$ respectively hence the correct order of base strength will be
$$
\mathrm{F}^{-} < \mathrm{NO}_2^{-} < \mathrm{CN}^{-}
$$
$(\because$ stronger the acid weaker will be its conjugate base)
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></msub></math> for the reaction, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mtext>Fe</mtext><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>(</mo><mi>aq</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>+</mo><msub><mtext>2H</mtext><mn>2</mn></msub><mtext>O</mtext><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>⇌</mo><mtext>Fe</mtext><mo>(</mo><mi>OH</mi><msup><mo>)</mo><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>(</mo><mi>aq</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>+</mo><msub><mtext>H</mtext><mn>3</mn></msub><msup><mtext>O</mtext><mo>+</mo></msup><mi>is</mi><mo> </mo><mo>6.5</mo><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></msup></math>. What is the maximum <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>pH</mi></math> value, which could be used, so that at least <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>80</mn><mo>%</mo></math> of the total iron <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi>III</mi></mfenced></math> in a dilute solution exist as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math>?
questions.solution:
This problem is based on the conceptual mixing of equilibrium constant and pH of the solution and given that,

Fe3+aq+2H2Ol⇌Fe(OH)2+aq+H3O+aq

Ka=6.5×10-4 and 80% of Fe3+ present in the final solution means only 20% is dissociated.

So, Fe3+ =0.8 Molar and FeOH2+=0.2 Molar

        Now, Ka=FeOH2+H3O+Fe3+

6.5×10-4=0.200.80H3O+

H3O+=6.5×10-4×4

H3O+=26.0×10-4=2.6×10-3

     pH=-logH3O+

            =-log2.6×10-3

             = 3 - log 2.6 = 2.59
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A solution of $\mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{Cl}$ and $\mathrm{NH}_{3}$ has $\mathrm{pH}=8.0 .$ Which of the following hydroxides may be precipitated when this solution is mixed with equal volume of 0.2 M of metal ion.
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{pH}=8, \mathrm{pOH}=6 ;\left[\mathrm{OH}^{-}\right]=10^{-6} \mathrm{M}$
Ionic product of $\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}=0.2 \times\left(1 \times 10^{-6}\right)^{2}$
$=2 \times 10^{-13}>\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{sp}}\left(=8.1 \times 10^{-16}\right)$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
